description_long: This object is a short, cylindrical stone obelisk with a slightly domed top, set on a square stone platform. The surface of the cylinder is weathered, showing signs of age and exposure, with some discoloration and moss at the base. The top of the obelisk features a radial pattern of shallow grooves, and there is a dark, rectangular plaque or inscription band on one side. The base platform is flat, made of a different, darker stone, and is large enough to provide stability. One side of the cylinder has a faint rectangular outline, possibly indicating a sealed or decorative panel. The overall appearance suggests it is a commemorative or functional urban marker, likely found in parks or on sidewalks.
description: A short, cylindrical stone obelisk with a domed, grooved top and a square stone base, featuring a dark plaque and weathered surface.
description_view_0: Front view shows the cylindrical body, domed top with grooves, and a dark plaque or inscription band clearly visible.
description_view_1: This side reveals a faint rectangular outline on the cylinder, possibly a sealed panel, with the top grooves visible.
description_view_2: The rear view displays the plain, weathered cylindrical surface and the grooved top, with no plaques or panels visible.
description_view_3: This angle shows both the faint rectangular outline and a partial view of the dark plaque, highlighting surface wear.
attribute_inscription: A dark, rectangular plaque or band is visible on the front side, likely containing an inscription, but the text is not legible in the images.
attribute_ornamentation: None visible aside from the radial grooves on the top surface.
attribute_base_design: The base is a flat, square platform made of dark stone, with a smooth surface and no visible decorative features.
attribute_tip_design: The tip is a gently domed top with shallow, radial grooves extending outward, giving a textured appearance.
